articles.title: 'Govt Ready To Take Everyone Forward'
articles.description: BHUPENDRA YADAV highlighted the government's vision for inclusive growth, stressing support for industry and the private sector to drive job creation. The goal is to empower businesses, boost economic development, and help transform India into the world's largest economy."

articles.title: ₹1.25 lakh per month, 25,000 meal allowance: Emirates cabin crew shares salary breakdown in viral video
articles.description: The creator also noted that the amount can vary every month based on flying hours.

articles.title: Key Cabinet decisions: Rs 39,290 crore package get govt nod; ATF fund, coastal highway and Delhi-NCR vehicle scheme on list
articles.description: The Union Cabinet approved a Rs 39,290 crore package for aviation, highways, and transport modernization. A significant Rs 10,000 crore was allocated for an Aviation Turbine Fuel Price Stabilisation Fund to manage fuel cost volatility.

articles.title: No More Jet Fuel Shocks? Inside The Rs 10,000 Crore Safety Net For Indian Airlines
articles.description: International ATF prices have surged nearly 2.5 times in recent months, rising from Rs 60.5 per litre in March 2026 to Rs 142 per litre in May 2026.

articles.title: A guide to using a Credit Card EMI calculator for planned purchases
articles.description: Before converting a purchase into EMIs, understand how a Credit Card EMI calculator can help you evaluate costs and plan your finances with greater clarity.

articles.title: Borosil Renewables climbs after govt levies CVD on Malaysian solar glass imports
articles.description: Borosil Renewables rallied 8.84% to Rs 545.80 after the the Government imposed a countervailing duty (CVD) on Textured Toughened (Tempered) Glass made or exported from Malaysia for a period of five years.

articles.title: Did RBI sell gold worth $12000000000 amid Iran War pressure on oil markets, economies? Govt says...
articles.description: The Central government has dismissed reports claiming that the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) sold gold worth 12 billion dollars to strengthen the country's foreign exchange reserves. The clarification came after a Bloomberg report suggested that the RBI may have reduced part of its gold holdings to shield foreign-currency reserves from the impact of the ongoing conflict in West Asia. In an official statement issued on June 3, the RBI categorically rejected the reports, stating that its physical gold holdings remain unchanged at 880.52 tonnes.

articles.title: Cabinet approves ₹10,000 crore fund to stabilise ATF costs amid crisis
articles.description: OMCs will be compensated for losses arising from elevated international ATF prices whenever the prevailing import parity price exceeds a benchmark price determined by the government

articles.title: SBI Chief Signals Likely RBI Rate Pause As Markets Eye Crucial MPC Decision
articles.description: SBI Chief Signals Likely RBI Rate Pause As Markets Eye Crucial MPC Decision

articles.title: Scottish EV firm Munro plans UK factory for 2027 – report
articles.description: The plant would be oriented towards domestic client demand, with output eventually scaling to thousands of vehicles per year.

articles.title: Putin's Russian Davos opens under war's shadow
articles.description: Russia hosts its premier investment forum in Saint Petersburg. The event includes prominent figures from the United States and Germany. This gathering occurs as Russia faces economic stagnation and ongoing conflict in Ukraine. The forum aims for pragmatic dialogue on a stable future. Discussions will cover energy, artificial intelligence, and investment.

articles.title: NHPC OFS Opens for Retail Investors Today: Should You Apply? Here's What Analysts Say
articles.description: The floor price for the NHPC OFS has been fixed at Rs 71 per share. With this, the OFS is available at a discount to the prevailing market price of around Rs 75 apiece.

articles.title: JioStar bets big on AI-generated shows after success of Mahabharat
articles.description: JioStar, a joint venture between Reliance Industries Ltd. and Walt Disney Co., is planning a slate of series that would be written, animated, voiced and edited entirely by artificial intelligence

articles.title: Cabinet approves Rs 10,000 crore for ATF price stabilization fund
articles.description: The Union Cabinet has approved a Rs 10,000 crore Aviation Turbine Fuel Price Stabilisation Fund. This fund aims to protect airlines and passengers from the sharp rise in ATF prices. The ongoing West Asia crisis has significantly impacted aviation fuel costs. This initiative will help manage the financial strain on the sector.

articles.title: Uday Kotak: Time to focus on business
articles.description: Uday Kotak, Founder and Director of Kotak Mahindra Bank, emphasized that it is time for India to focus on the business of business now that other distractions have concluded. This call to action comes as global technology giants scale up their financial capacity to build next-generation infrastructure, highlighting a massive scale gap between global tech corporations and the Indian corporate landscape.

articles.title: CMR Green IPO: Market leader riding the green aluminium wave
articles.description: Market leadership, liquid aluminium capability, and recycling tailwinds make this IPO a compelling green manufacturing play

articles.description: Mumbai : Hinduja Renewables Energy Private Limited (HREPL), the renewable energy Company of the Hinduja Group, has signed solar Power Delivery Agreements with Höganäs India Private Limited and Hirschvogel Components India Private Limited. The agreements will support industrial decarbonisation by scaling integrated renewable solutions across India’s manufacturing clusters. articles.description: John Cockerill India, Advait Energy Transitions, Bliss GVS Pharma, and Concord Biotech were among the stocks in focus on Wednesday following key corporate developments

articles.title: Godrej starts wealth management business
articles.description: Godrej Industries Group has unveiled its new wealth management venture, Godrej Wealth, aiming for a substantial Rs 1,00,000 crore in assets under management by 2031. This expansion into financial services targets affluent clients with investable assets of Rs 2 crore and above, offering comprehensive services including succession planning and global investments.

articles.title: Wealth Co enters pact with NSE for EGR
articles.description: India Business News: MUMBAI: The Wealth Co, the assets management arm of domestic financial services group Pantomath, on Tuesday signed an agreement with NSE to offer Elec.

articles.title: Kotak prods India Inc to start investing again
articles.description: Billionaire Uday Kotak urged Indian businesses to invest in the future, highlighting a global lag in AI technology. He cited Google's massive $80 billion AI infrastructure investment, noting its scale dwarfs India's entire listed market. Kotak called this a wake-up call for companies to prioritize future growth, especially after the IPL season concludes, emphasizing the 'business of business'.

articles.title: India slips to seventh in global market capitalisation ranking
articles.description: India has dropped to seventh in global stock market valuation, overtaken by South Korea and Taiwan. This decline, attributed to sustained foreign fund selling and muted corporate earnings, contrasts with the AI chip leadership of South Korean and Taiwanese firms. Despite a recent Sensex uptick driven by domestic buying, foreign investors remain net sellers, impacting India's market cap.

articles.title: India, UK to narrow gaps to roll out trade deal
articles.description: India and the UK are working to resolve outstanding issues, including steel quotas and duties, to expedite the implementation of their bilateral trade agreement. Discussions between Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and UK Secretary of State Peter Kyle focused on overcoming these sticking points to advance economic engagement and strengthen the partnership.

articles.title: Government plans new indices to track informal mfg, services
articles.description: The government is developing new indices to measure industrial and service sector production in India's vast informal economy. These will draw from extensive surveys to provide a clearer picture of the unorganized sector, moving beyond proxy methods that distorted data, especially post-pandemic. The new metrics aim to improve policymaking for millions reliant on this sector.

articles.title: Texas Instruments names insider Julie Knecht as finance chief
articles.description: June 2 : Texas Instruments on Tuesday named insider Julie Knecht as its finance chief, succeeding Rafael Lizardi, who will retire after 25 years with the analog chipmaker.Knecht, currently chief accounting officer and vice president of accounting and tax, will assume the role on August 1, while Lizardi will t

articles.title: Despite extensive U.S. trade surplus, Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil
articles.description: WASHINGTON — The Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil, charging that the world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in trade practices that are “unreasonable’’ and that “burden or restrict U.S. commerce.’’ Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said he received the decision “with indignation.” He also blamed the decision ...

articles.title: Citi: India's AI advantage needs better articulation
articles.description: Citi's Vis Raghavan notes muted FDI in India due to energy dependency and AI uncertainty, despite strong domestic sentiment. He highlights the need for India to articulate its AI-driven global economy position, emphasizing its cost advantage in compute and potential as a global AI hub.

articles.title: Threats to America's prosperity begins in Washington
articles.description: The economy remains solid. From 2016 to 2024, annual gross domestic product growth averaged 2.5%. After a rocky first quarter, the balance of 2025 matched that performance. Despite the war, 2% growth is still likely this year. Adjusted for inflation, we have survived significantly higher gasoline prices, and President Trump’s impact on the ...

articles.title: DCCB Visakhapatnam posts robust growth, net profit jumps 82%
articles.description: DCCB Visakhapatnam reports 82% net profit increase, driven by diversified lending and improved asset quality for fiscal 2026.

articles.title: Wipro to up its stake in U.S. firm Aggne Global to 80%
articles.description: Wipro plans to increase its stake in Aggne Global to 80% for $28.5 million, enhancing its insurance sector capabilities.

articles.description: President Trump has replaced one-third of his senior White House staff, including four Cabinet secretaries, since returning to office. It's a remarkably stable pace compared to Mr. Trump's first term. It also puts him on par with other modern presidents. Nearly a year and a half into Mr. Trump's second term, senior ...

articles.description: CRRI recommends enhanced traffic load standards for Amaravati roads to ensure durability and performance in capital-region infrastructure.

articles.description: AIUTUC urges immediate implementation of revised wages for workers in Kalaburagi, amid ongoing economic hardships and protests.

articles.title: In Mumbai, founders don't burn money; they make each rupee count: Naiyya Saggi
articles.description: Naiyya Saggi makes the case that Mumbai isn't just a finance hub; it's home to one of India's largest and most profitable tech ecosystems. Four of the five biggest companies by market cap sit here, alongside BFSI, media, consumer and manufacturing, plus the closest access to capital, consumers and customers in the country. Her argument: Mumbai founders build with fundamentals, respect profitability, and make every rupee count.

articles.description: There is a particular kind of disappointment that only middle age, airline loyalty programmes and a modest amount of disposable income can produce. It usually arrives somewhere around Row 6. Not Row 1, where people...

articles.title: APCRDA holds public hearing on land acquisition for Amaravati projects
articles.description: APCRDA holds a public hearing on land acquisition for Amaravati, offering farmers options for land pooling and infrastructure development.

articles.description: TASL Defence MRO: Tata Sons' Chairman reviews TASL's new MRO facility in Bengaluru for advanced defence systems and strategic development.

articles.description: Sivaganga farmers demand immediate re-examination of Old Vaigai irrigation area to ensure fair water distribution and protect agriculture.

articles.title: AI revival infuses life in Indian IT shares: Nifty IT jumps 8% in 3 days, Infosys up 10% as demand fears ease
articles.description: The Nifty IT index has surged nearly 8 percent in 3 sessions as investors realise that the artificial intelligence (AI) boom could prove to be an opportunity rather than a threat. Concerns that AI could disrupt traditional software business models have begun to ease.

articles.description: SK Hynix will do whatever it requires to fund that expansion in chip wafer capacity, Chey said.

articles.description: Silver prices climbed over Rs 5,000 to Rs 2.71 lakh per kg on MCX, tracking strong global gains as investors await key US economic data and Fed cues.

articles.title: Adani Group cos post record $16 bn capex, all-time high EBITDA of Rs 94,834 cr in FY26
articles.description: Adani Group companies achieved a record capital expenditure of Rs 1.53 lakh crore and an all-time high EBITDA of Rs 94,834 crore in FY26, fueling infrastructure expansion. Nearly 80% of investments targeted energy, utilities, transport, and logistics, with leverage maintained below the 3.5x target at 3.3x.

articles.title: Market wrap: Sensex rises 383 points, Nifty closes near 23,500 as IT stocks shine
articles.description: Indian stock market staged a sharp recovery, with Sensex and Nifty erasing morning losses to snap a four-session losing streak, led by a strong surge in IT stocks including heavyweight TCS, Infosys, HCL Tech and Tech Mahindra.

articles.title: US banks rely on a 65-year-old programming language; companies are paying a premium for developers who know it
articles.description: A 1959 programming language, COBOL, is powering global finance and government systems. As experienced programmers retire, companies face a talent crisis. This has created a lucrative opportunity for new developers learning this older skill. Banks and agencies are paying top salaries to maintain these vital systems. AI is also being explored to help manage COBOL code.

articles.title: July DA hike: DA at 63%? How much more salary and pension could you get?
articles.description: A 3% Dearness Allowance (DA) hike from July 2026 is looking increasingly likely, with the latest inflation data indicating that DA could rise from 60% to 63%. If approved, the move would boost the salaries and pensions of more than 1.2 crore central government employees and pensioners.

articles.title: From Rs 2 lakh to Rs 57 lakh: KPMG employee shares how his salary jumped 26 times in 10 years
articles.description: Anirudh Rapole, a chartered accountant, shared his decade-long salary journey at KPMG. Starting with less than ₹2 lakh annually during his Articleship, his earnings grew significantly. A move to KPMG Sweden in 2021 saw his salary jump to over ₹57 lakh. Rapole emphasized CA as an equalizer, enabling growth through hard work.
